old_value: {"id":"project-LICHEN-8b8u","title":"[cross-impl] IID derivation diverges between Rust and Python/C","description":"Rust (lichen-link/src/identity.rs:24) uses keyed hash_32 twice per project-LICHEN-swvz update. Python (crypto/identity.py:138: sha256(pubkey).digest()[:8]) and C (lichen/subsys/lichen/l2/ipv6_addr.c:90: lichen_sha256 + memcpy first 8 bytes) both follow SHA-256(pubkey)[:8] + clear U/L bit. spec/03-addressing.md:15-16 specifies the SHA-256 method as source of truth. Breaks human_address, IPv6 IID consistency, and interop. Test vectors for addresses will fail across langs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"agent@lichen-project.dev","created_at":"2026-07-22T21:19:29.088734245Z","created_by":"AI Agent","updated_at":"2026-07-22T21:19:29.088734245Z"}
